perturbation.original_text: PROVIDED that the obligations herein undertaken will not apply to: a) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which after disclosure becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the recipient, or b) information which the recipient party can show was in its possession at the time of disclosure or which is independently developed by the recipient and wa s not acquired directly or indirectly from the disclosing party, or c) information which is made public at any time by the disclosing party, or by others with the permission of the disclosing party, or d) information which is received by the receivi ng party from a third party without similar restriction and without breach of this Agreement. e) information which is required to be disclosed by legal process, law or regulatory authority.
perturbation.changed_text: PROVIDED that the obligations herein undertaken will not apply to: a) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which after disclosure becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the recipient, or b) information which the recipient party can show was in its possession at the time of disclosure or which is independently developed by the recipient and wa s acquired directly or indirectly from the disclosing party, or c) information which is made public at any time by the disclosing party, or by others with the permission of the disclosing party, or d) information which is received by the receivi ng party from a third party without similar restriction and without breach of this Agreement. e) information which is required to be disclosed by legal process, law or regulatory authority.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "not acquired" to "acquired" in clause b, we now state that the exemption to confidentiality applies even to information independently developed BUT acquired from the disclosing party. This is contradictory. The agreement aims to protect information disclosed, but this change implies even independently developed information is exempt if the recipient acquired it from the disclosing party, even though it was developed independently. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 4. The original intent of the confidentiality agreement is now undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROVIDED that the obligations herein undertaken will not apply to: a) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which after disclosure becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the recipient, or b) information which the recipient party can show was in its possession at the time of disclosure or which is independently developed by the recipient and wa s acquired directly or indirectly from the disclosing party, or c) information which is made public at any time by the disclosing party, or by others with the permission of the disclosing party, or d) information which is received by the receivi ng party from a third party without similar restriction and without breach of this Agreement. e) information which is required to be disclosed by legal process, law or regulatory authority.
perturbation.justification: The change from "not acquired" to "acquired" introduces a direct contradiction. The original text exempts information independently developed AND not acquired from the disclosing party from confidentiality obligations. The changed text now includes information independently developed BUT acquired from the disclosing party, which directly undermines the confidentiality agreement's purpose of protecting the disclosing party's information, even if independently developed. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, as it creates a logical inconsistency and uncertainty about which information is protected, therefore the Contradicted Text contradicts Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement is to be construed and enforced in accordance with English Law and is subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts to which the parties hereto submit. This clause shall not prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement is to be construed and enforced in accordance with English Law, except for matters regarding confidentiality breaches, which will be subject to binding arbitration in a neutral country. This clause shall not prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the dispute resolution mechanism. Initially, disputes were subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of English courts. Now, confidentiality breaches are subject to binding arbitration in a neutral country. This contradicts the original clause's provision for English court jurisdiction, introducing uncertainty about the correct venue for dispute resolution. This creates a contradiction within Section 6 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement is to be construed and enforced in accordance with English Law, except for matters regarding confidentiality breaches, which will be subject to binding arbitration in a neutral country. This clause shall not prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction of English courts for all matters. The changed text carves out an exception for confidentiality breaches, subjecting them to binding arbitration in a neutral country. This directly contradicts the exclusive jurisdiction clause, introducing two different dispute resolution mechanisms within the same section, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as the same Section 6 now includes conflicting requirements.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, or the copyright subsisting in them.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, except under special circumstances that a transfer of copyright might be granted. or the copyright subsisting in them.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity around ownership. The original text explicitly states that NO transfer of ownership occurs. The modified text adds "except under special circumstances that a transfer of copyright might be granted". This directly contradicts the first part of the sentence and casts doubt on who owns the documents or software, making the text ambiguous. There is an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, except under special circumstances that a transfer of copyright might be granted. or the copyright subsisting in them.
perturbation.justification: The original text unequivocally prohibits ownership transfer. The changed text introduces an exception for 'special circumstances', creating a contradiction. The modified section 4 now includes this contradiction and the contradicted section is exactly the same as the changed section, highlighting that the change introduced the contradiction, based on the explanation.
